207,simple,MK-TER80-26,,"Minnkota Terrova 80",1,0,visible,"A GPS-enabled trolling motor with spot-lock technology. Set your position and fish hands-free.","

The Minnkota Terrova 80 is the trolling motor serious anglers choose. With 80 lbs of thrust and built-in i-Pilot GPS, you get Spot-Lock anchor technology that holds your boat within 5 feet of a waypoint, even in wind and current.

\n

The brushless, variable-speed motor runs quieter and more efficiently than conventional trolling motors, and the integrated Bluetooth lets you control everything from your smartphone. AutoPilot maintains a heading so you can focus on fishing, not steering.

\n

Vertical jigging for walleye, working a weed edge for bass, the Terrova 80 makes you a more effective angler. Stop in to see it mounted on our demo boat.

",,,taxable,,backorder,,,0,0,,,,,0,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,0,"Minn Kota",MK-TER80-26,2026,new,on_order,"Thrust

column2: Brushless",1,"Once you fish with Spot-Lock you'll never go back. The Terrova 80 is the most-requested trolling motor upgrade we do. Pairs perfectly with any of our Princecraft fishing boats."

214,simple,HS-STRK250R-26,,"Hisun Strike 250R — Youth Sport UTV",1,0,visible,"Youth/entry sport UTV. Two-seat, governed for teen riders, four factory colour options.","

The Hisun Strike 250R is the sport UTV we point families at when they want a two-seat trail machine sized for a teen rider and a passenger. Governed speed and manageable power make it a legitimate teaching machine — a step up from a single-rider ATV into proper side-by-side trail skills.

\n

Available in four factory finishes (Black Ops, Destroyer Grey, Tactical Tan, Voodoo Blue). Sport suspension, real ground clearance, and full Hisun dealer support through Reyco. Warranty service and parts handled right here in Sault Ste. Marie.

\n

Most common use case: Algoma families with teens starting on OFATV trails under adult supervision. Pre-order deposits accepted; ask us about current stock and ordering lead times.

",,,taxable,,backorder,,,0,0,,,,,0,,,,,,,http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/800_stike250blue-110557.png,,,,,,,,,0,Hisun,HS-STRK250R-26,2026,new,on_order,"Engine

column2: Black Ops, Destroyer Grey, Tactical Tan, Voodoo Blue

Hisun Manufacturer",1,"The Strike 250R is the youth sport UTV I actually recommend. Governed power means kids learn right instead of getting over their head. Parents ride along and teach. Nothing else in this price band does it better."

224,simple,EC-CS590-26,,"Echo CS-590 Timber Wolf Chainsaw",1,0,visible,"The firewood cutter's chainsaw. 59.8cc pro-grade, 20"" bar, legendary reliability.","

The CS-590 Timber Wolf is the chainsaw that cuts most of the firewood in Northern Ontario. It earned that reputation — 59.8cc of pro-grade engine, a 20"" bar that handles full-size hardwood, and the durability that comes from Echo's professional-duty engineering. Ask any firewood cutter from the Sault to Wawa what saw they run; the answer is almost always this one.

\n

Anti-vibration handle reduces fatigue on long cutting days. Automatic oiler keeps the chain lubricated. Decompression valve makes starting easier — especially on cold mornings when you need to get to work. The kind of details Echo engineers get right.

\n

We stock CS-590 chains, bars, filters, and wear parts on the shelf. When your Timber Wolf needs service, we handle it here at the shop. Most-sold chainsaw at Reyco.

",,,taxable,,backorder,,,0,0,,,,,0,,,,,,,http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/EC-CS590-26.jpg,,,,,,,,,0,Echo,EC-CS590-26,2026,new,on_order,"Engine

column2: 5-Year Consumer / 2-Year Commercial",1,"CS-590 Timber Wolf has probably cut more firewood across Algoma than every other saw combined. When pro customers ask what they should run, this is the first answer. Reliable, powerful, and the parts are everywhere."

225,simple,EC-CS310-26,,"Echo CS-310 Chainsaw",1,0,visible,"Homeowner entry chainsaw. 30.5cc, 14"" bar, reliable light-duty saw.","

The Echo CS-310 is the homeowner chainsaw we point budget-conscious customers at. 30.5cc engine and 14"" bar handle light firewood, storm cleanup, and occasional brush work. Not meant for production firewood cutting, but solid for the weekend property owner doing 2-3 cords a year.

\n

Lightweight, easy-start carb, automatic oiler. Proven Echo reliability at entry pricing. Casey would recommend this over cheaper off-brand saws every time.

\n

Full parts availability and in-house service. Chains, bars, air filters all stocked.

",,,taxable,,backorder,,,0,0,,,,,0,,,,,,,http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/EC-CS310-26.jpg,,,,,,,,,0,Echo,EC-CS310-26,2026,new,on_order,"Engine

column2: 5-Year Consumer",1,"CS-310 is the right saw for someone cutting 2-3 cords a year or doing occasional storm cleanup. Not a firewood production saw, but reliable for the homeowner use case at an honest price."

226,simple,EC-CS352-26,,"Echo CS-352 Chainsaw",1,0,visible,"Mid-homeowner chainsaw. 34.4cc, 16"" bar, good all-around value.","

The CS-352 steps up from the CS-310 — 34.4cc engine and a 16"" bar for slightly heavier work. Right saw for the property owner doing 4-6 cords of firewood a year, clearing storm damage, or maintaining trails. The best value in Echo's mid-homeowner lineup.

\n

Easy-start carb, automatic chain oiler, anti-vibration handle. Holds up under regular seasonal use.

\n

Reyco stocks parts and handles service in-house. Common upgrade from customers who started with the CS-310 and found they needed more saw.

",,,taxable,,backorder,,,0,0,,,,,0,,,,,,,http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/EC-CS352-26.jpg,,,,,,,,,0,Echo,EC-CS352-26,2026,new,on_order,"Engine

column2: 5-Year Consumer",1,"CS-352 is the Goldilocks homeowner saw — not too big, not too small. For 4-6 cords a year plus storm work, it's the right fit."

227,simple,EC-CS680-26,,"Echo CS-680 Chainsaw",1,0,visible,"Heavy pro chainsaw. 66.8cc, 24"" bar, production-grade.","

The CS-680 is pro-grade territory. 66.8cc and a 24"" bar for heavy firewood production, larger timber work, and professional landscape/tree crews. If you're cutting more than 10 cords a year or running the saw for commercial work, this is where Echo's pro engineering pays off.

\n

Anti-vibration, automatic oiler, decompression valve, magnesium crankcase. Built for long cutting days and hard work.

\n

In-house service for warranty and non-warranty work. Pro parts (chains, bars, sprockets, clutches) stocked for the CS-680 specifically.

",,,taxable,,backorder,,,0,0,,,,,0,,,,,,,http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/EC-CS680-26.jpg,,,,,,,,,0,Echo,EC-CS680-26,2026,new,on_order,"Engine

column2: 5-Year Consumer / 2-Year Commercial",1,"CS-680 is where you go when the CS-590 isn't enough. Production firewood, large timber, commercial work. The magnesium crankcase and pro duty cycle make it hold up where lesser saws wear out."

column1: 1034,simple,,,"2023 Princecraft Sportfisher 21-2S Fishing Pontoon",1,0,visible,"21-foot fishing pontoon with dedicated casting platforms, livewell, and rod storage. The pontoon for anglers who want stability and deck space without giving up fishing-specific features.","The 2023 Princecraft Sportfisher 21 2S is the fishing pontoon for anglers who don't want to choose between a stable platform and serious fishing features. The Sportfisher line is Princecraft's dedicated fishing pontoon, distinct from their general-purpose pontoon lineup. Where typical pontoons are built for cruising and entertaining, the Sportfisher is laid out for casting, trolling, and landing fish.

\nThe hull is twin aluminum pontoon tubes built to Princecraft's pontoon-construction standard, engineered for the chop and wakes of bigger Northern Ontario lakes. The 21-foot length gives you the deck space for multiple anglers without the docking and trailering complexity of the longer 23 and 25-foot variants.

\nFishing-specific layout is what makes the Sportfisher worth the price difference over a base pontoon. Dedicated front and rear casting decks give two anglers room to work in any wind direction. The integrated livewell is sized for a serious day's catch, not the afterthought tank you find on cruising pontoons. Rod storage is built-in, not aftermarket-mounted. Trolling-friendly seat positioning lets you fish from anywhere on the boat without rearranging furniture.

\nThe pontoon platform is what shifts the calculation versus a deep-V fishing boat. Stability for kids, older anglers, or anyone who's done with rolling around in a 17-footer. Deck space for shore lunches, cooler stowage, and equipment that doesn't fit on a traditional fishing hull. And it still fishes hard, because Princecraft built the Sportfisher line specifically for that use case.
\nThe 2S designation indicates the specific trim package; check with Reyco for exact engine, electronics, and accessory configuration included.

\nAvailable at Reyco Marine and Small Engine Ltd. in Sault Ste. Marie. Financing through Reyco's standard partners; rigging, motor installation, and electronics setup handled in-house.",,,taxable,,1,,,0,0,,,,,1,,,,Uncategorized,,,"http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/AttachmentImage-2.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/AttachmentImage.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/AttachmentImage-1.jpg",,,,,,,,,0,Princecraft,34493,2023,new,in_stock,"Make

column2: B",2,"The Sportfisher pontoon is what I sell to anglers who used to fish hard from a deep-V and are ready for something more comfortable without giving up the fishing. The 21-footer is the size that works for most Northern Ontario applications: big enough for Lake Superior bays in fair weather, manageable for trailering, and stable enough that grandkids and older relatives can come along. What separates the Sportfisher from a regular pontoon is the layout. Cruising pontoons have the chairs in the wrong places for fishing. The Sportfisher has casting platforms where you actually need them, livewell where it should be, rod storage that isn't an afterthought. It fishes like a fishing boat but you've got the deck space of a pontoon. For families who want one boat that fishes serious and entertains comfortably, this is the answer in the 21-foot range. Match it with a trolling motor, a fishfinder, and the right outboard, and you've got a setup that handles 80 percent of what most anglers in this market actually do. 1038,simple,,,"2026 Cub Cadet XT1 Enduro Series ST54 FAB Riding Mower",1,0,visible,"54-inch fabricated deck riding mower with 24 HP V-Twin engine, hydrostatic transmission, and 12-position cutting height. Built for properties up to 5 acres.","The Cub Cadet XT1 ST54 FAB is the largest deck in the XT1 Enduro Series, sized for properties that take a 42-inch tractor too long to mow. The 54-inch fabricated 12-gauge steel deck cuts a wide swath that takes a 3 to 5-acre lawn from a 3-hour job to under 90 minutes. Where stamped decks dent on the first hidden rock or root, the FAB deck shrugs off impact and stays straight season after season.

\nPower comes from a 24 HP V-Twin OHV engine, sized correctly for the 54-inch cutting width. Smaller engines bog down on wide decks in tall grass; this one delivers consistent torque under load. The hydrostatic transmission gives smooth pedal-controlled forward and reverse, and the included cruise control holds your speed across the long straight passes that come with bigger lawns.

\nThe 16-inch SmartJet steering wheel paired with a tight turning radius keeps the larger machine maneuverable around obstacles. Twelve cutting positions from 1 to 4 inches cover the standard residential range, with single-lever adjustment from the seat.

\nWhat sets the XT1 Enduro Series apart from lower trims in this size class is the chassis design. The XT1 frame is built for the 5-year chassis warranty Cub Cadet stands behind on the Enduro Series. Combined with the 3-year limited consumer warranty, the part most likely to fail under sustained load is covered for the long haul.

\nIf your lawn is under an acre, this is too much machine. For 3 to 5 acres of mostly open ground, this is the right answer at the XT1 trim level. Available at Reyco Marine and Small Engine Ltd. in Sault Ste. Marie. Financing through Reyco's standard partners; service and parts handled in-house by certified Cub Cadet technicians.",,,taxable,,1,,,0,0,,,,,1,,,,Uncategorized,,,"http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT1ST54_New_2000x2000_4.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT1ST54_New_2000x2000_1.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT1ST54_New_2000x2000_2.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT1ST54_New_2000x2000_3.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT1ST54_New_2000x2000_7-1.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT1ST54_New_2000x2000_8.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT1ST54_New_2000x2000_9.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT1ST54_New_2000x2000_6.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT1ST54_New_2000x2000_5.jpg",,,,,,,,,0,"Cub Cadet",13AQA2TMA10,2026,new,in_stock,"Make

column2: Tractor",4,"The ST54 FAB is the right machine for customers with bigger lawns who don't want to step up to a zero-turn. A 54-inch deck cuts a 3-acre property in a fraction of the time a 42-inch deck takes. The fab deck is the part that matters long-term. Stamped decks at this size bend the first season; this one stays straight. The 24 HP V-Twin handles the full 54-inch width without bogging in tall or wet grass. That's the biggest mistake I see customers make: putting a smaller engine on a wider deck. You end up slowing your ground speed every time the grass gets thicker, which kills the time savings of the bigger deck. This one is sized right. For 3 to 5 acres of mostly open lawn, this is the XT1 I recommend. If you've got more obstacles than open ground, drop down to the LT46 or step over to the XT2 for the cast-iron front axle. But for big open properties, the ST54 FAB is dollar-for-dollar the right machine in the Cub Cadet lineup.",57803,4899,4899,1I03SH20265,"XT1 ST54 column1: 1039,simple,,,"2026 Cub Cadet XT2 Enduro Series GX54 D Riding Mower",1,0,visible,"54-inch fabricated deck riding mower with 24 HP Kawasaki V-Twin engine, cast-iron front axle, and hydrostatic transmission. The GX-series Kawasaki upgrade for serious residential lawns.","The 2026 Cub Cadet XT2 GX54 D is the Kawasaki-powered upgrade in Cub Cadet's mid-tier Enduro Series. The 'GX' designation marks the engine swap from Cub Cadet's standard V-Twin to the Kawasaki FR691V, an industry workhorse known for longevity in commercial cutting applications. For homeowners who want a residential machine that reads as commercial-spec under the hood, this is the trim.

\nThe 54-inch fabricated 12-gauge steel deck handles 3 to 5-acre properties efficiently, with the wider cut path turning a 3-hour mowing job into 90 minutes. Combined with the Kawasaki's 24 HP output, the deck stays at full ground speed even in tall, wet, or thick grass that would slow lesser engines.

\nThe XT2 chassis adds a cast-iron front axle, the upgrade that separates the XT2 line from the XT1 entry tier. After 5 or 6 seasons of load, standard front axles develop play; the cast-iron version stays tight. The 16-inch SmartJet steering wheel with cruise control keeps long passes comfortable. LED headlights are standard, and the cushioned high-back seat with armrests makes a 2-hour mow far less punishing on the back.

\nTwelve cutting positions from 1 to 4 inches cover the full residential turf range, with single-lever adjustment from the seat. The 4-gallon fuel tank gives roughly 4 hours of continuous mowing, which matches the cushion of fuel a 3 to 5-acre lawn typically requires.

\nCub Cadet backs the XT2 Enduro Series with a 3-year limited consumer warranty plus a 5-year chassis warranty. With the Kawasaki engine specifically, you're looking at a power plant that commercial lawn services run for thousands of hours; in residential service, expect it to outlast the chassis.

\nAvailable at Reyco Marine and Small Engine Ltd. in Sault Ste. Marie. Financing through Reyco's standard partners; service and parts handled in-house by certified Cub Cadet technicians.",,,taxable,,1,,,0,0,,,,,1,,,,Uncategorized,,,"http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT2GX54_2000x2000_1.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT2GX54_2000x2000_2.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT2GX54_2000x2000_8.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT2GX54_2000x2000_7.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT2GX54_2000x2000_6.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT2GX54_2000x2000_5.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT2GX54_2000x2000_4.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT2GX54_2000x2000_3.jpg",,,,,,,,,0,"Cub Cadet",14B7A6TMA10,2026,new,in_stock,"Make

column2: Tractor",4,"The XT2 GX54 D is the riding mower I sell to customers who want commercial-grade engine quality without going to a full commercial-zero-turn machine. The Kawasaki FR691V is the engine you find on commercial walk-behind mowers running 8 hours a day; in a residential XT2, it'll outlast the chassis. That's the real value here. Cub Cadet's standard V-Twin is fine for a residential workload, but the Kawasaki gives you another 5 to 8 seasons of engine life on top of an already-good chassis. Customers who plan to keep their riding mower 10-plus years come out ahead on the GX trim. The 54-inch fab deck plus cast-iron front axle is the right combination for 3 to 5-acre properties. If your lawn is smaller than that, you don't need the 54-inch width. If it's larger or has serious slopes, we'd talk about stepping into a zero-turn instead. This is the smart upgrade for serious residential use. Not the cheapest trim, but the one that gives you the longest tail.",57805,6799,6799,1C27TH40004,"XT2 GX54 column1: 1041,simple,,,"2026 Cub Cadet XT2 Enduro Series LX46 Riding Mower",1,0,visible,"46-inch fabricated deck riding mower with 23 HP V-Twin engine, hydrostatic transmission, and cast-iron front axle. The mid-tier Enduro Series upgrade for properties up to 3 acres.","The 2026 Cub Cadet XT2 LX46 sits in the middle of Cub Cadet's residential lineup, a step up from the XT1 in chassis quality and operator comfort. The 46-inch fabricated deck cuts a wide path while staying narrow enough to maneuver through fence gates and around tight obstacles that wider 50-inch tractors can't clear.

\nPower comes from a 23 HP V-Twin OHV engine, sized for sustained-load mowing without bog-down in tall, wet, or thick grass. The hydrostatic transmission delivers smooth pedal-controlled forward and reverse, and the included PowerSmart cruise control holds your speed across long straight passes so your right foot doesn't tire on a 2-hour mowing day.

\nWhat separates the XT2 from the XT1 is the chassis. A cast-iron front axle replaces the standard one, adding both durability and steering feel under load. The LED headlights are standard, not optional, which matters more than you'd think when you're starting a mow at 7 PM in late summer. The higher-back seat with cushioned armrests is a real comfort upgrade for anyone who spends serious time in the saddle.

\nThe 16-inch SmartJet steering wheel paired with a tight turning radius gets you around trees and beds without lift-and-shift. Twelve cutting positions from 1 to 4 inches cover everything from putting-green-fine to standard residential turf.

\nCub Cadet backs the XT2 Enduro Series with a 3-year limited consumer warranty plus a 5-year chassis warranty, the same coverage as the higher-end XT3 line. The part most likely to fail under load is covered for the long haul.

\nAvailable at Reyco Marine and Small Engine Ltd. in Sault Ste. Marie. Financing through Reyco's standard partners; service and parts handled in-house by certified Cub Cadet technicians.",,,taxable,,1,,,0,0,,,,,1,,,,Uncategorized,,,"http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT2_LX46_2000x2000_6.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT2_LX46_2000x2000_1.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT2_LX46_2000x2000_2.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT2_LX46_2000x2000_5.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT2_LX46_2000x2000_4.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT2_LX46_2000x2000_3.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT2_LX46_2000x2000_7.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cub-Cadet_XT2_LX46_2000x2000_8.jpg",,,,,,,,,0,"Cub Cadet",13APA1TNA10,2026,new,in_stock,"Make

column2: Tractor",4,"The XT2 LX46 is the upgrade I recommend when a customer's first riding mower is wearing out and they want something that lasts longer this time. The cast-iron front axle is the main reason. After 5 or 6 seasons the front end on a standard tractor starts to play; the cast iron just doesn't. The 46-inch deck is the size most homeowners actually need. Wide enough to cut fast, narrow enough to fit through gates and into sheds. I have customers who tried the 50 and 54-inch tractors first and downsized after they realized the wider deck was costing them access to half their yard. LED headlights are standard, which sounds like a small thing until you've finished a mow at dusk. Cruise control matters when you're doing long straight passes for an hour. And the cushioned seat is the kind of upgrade you don't think you need until you've sat on one. For 1 to 3 acres of residential lawn with some trees and beds to navigate, this is the machine I put homeowners on more than any other in the lineup.",57686,5299,5299,1B18TH20190,"XT2 column1: 1042,simple,,,"2026 Cub Cadet XT1 Enduro Series LT42 IntelliPOWER Riding Mower",1,0,visible,"42-inch deck riding mower with the IntelliPOWER V-Twin engine, hydrostatic transmission, and 12-position cutting height. Built for properties up to 2 acres.","The Cub Cadet XT1 LT42 IntelliPOWER is the entry into Cub Cadet's Enduro Series, sized for the most common residential lawn footprint. The 42-inch stamped 13-gauge steel deck cuts a wide path while staying compact enough to fit through fence gates and into standard sheds, a practical consideration that wider tractors don't share.

\nWhat sets this trim apart is the IntelliPOWER engine. Cub Cadet's branded V-Twin OHV runs at 21 HP, but the 'IntelliPOWER' designation refers to the electronic governor system that adjusts engine output based on load. When you hit a thicker patch of grass or a slope, the engine compensates automatically without bog-down. It's a meaningful improvement over fixed-output engines in the same horsepower class.

\nThe hydrostatic transmission delivers smooth pedal-controlled forward and reverse, which matters most when you're maneuvering around obstacles. Twelve cutting positions from 1 to 4 inches cover the standard residential turf range. The 16-inch SmartJet steering wheel paired with a tight turning radius keeps you mobile around trees, beds, and play structures.

\nThe XT1 chassis is built for the Enduro Series longevity standard. Cub Cadet backs it with a 3-year limited consumer warranty and a 5-year chassis warranty, the same coverage as their higher-end lines. For homeowners managing 1 to 2 acres who want a reliable machine without paying for the fab deck or cast-iron front axle of the XT2, this is the right answer.

\nAvailable at Reyco Marine and Small Engine Ltd. in Sault Ste. Marie. column1: 1058,simple,,,"2026 Cub Cadet XT1 Enduro Series LT50 FAB Riding Mower",1,0,visible,"50-inch fabricated deck riding mower with 24 HP V-Twin engine, hydrostatic transmission, and 12-position cutting height. Built for properties up to 3 acres.","The 2026 Cub Cadet XT1 LT50 FAB delivers commercial-grade durability in a residential riding mower. At its heart is a 50-inch fabricated 12-gauge steel deck. That deck is heavier and more impact-resistant than the stamped decks found on most lawn tractors in this class. Whether you're maintaining a 1-acre lawn or a 3-acre property, the wider cut path and reinforced deck shell shave both mowing time and long-term wear.

\nPower comes from a 24 HP V-Twin OHV engine, designed for sustained-load mowing in tall or wet grass without the bog-down common in single-cylinder competitors. The hydrostatic transmission means smooth, pedal-controlled forward and reverse, so there's no shifting and no jolts when changing direction at row ends. Cruise control holds your speed across straight passes, which matters more than you'd think when you're mowing for two hours straight.

\nThe 16-inch SmartJet steering wheel paired with a tight 16-inch turning radius gets you around trees, garden beds, and play structures without lift-and-shift. Twelve cutting positions from 1 to 4 inches cover everything from putting-green-fine to standard turf height.

\nWhat sets the XT1 Enduro Series apart from the LX or LT lower tiers is the chassis. Thicker steel frame, heavier-duty front axle, and the upgraded fab deck. Cub Cadet backs this with a 3-year limited consumer warranty plus a 5-year chassis warranty on the Enduro Series. The part most likely to fail under load is covered for the long haul.

\nAvailable at Reyco Marine and Small Engine Ltd. in Sault Ste. Marie. column1: 1059,simple,,,"2026 Cub Cadet CC30H Compact Rear-Engine Riding Mower",1,0,visible,"30-inch compact riding mower with hydrostatic drive. Fits through a 36-inch garden gate and turns in tight spaces. Built for lawns under 1 acre.","The 2026 Cub Cadet CC30H solves the problem most riding mowers don't: lawns that are too big for a push mower but too tight for a full-size lawn tractor. The 30-inch single-blade deck cuts a wide-enough swath to clear a half-acre in well under an hour, but the rear-engine design keeps the overall footprint short enough to fit through a standard 36-inch garden gate. If your back yard is fenced, this is the mower that gets through.

\nPower comes from a 10.5 HP single-cylinder OHV engine, sized appropriately for the deck width. The hydrostatic transmission (the H in CC30H) means you control speed with a foot pedal instead of a shifter. Smooth, intuitive, and forgiving when you're maneuvering around trees, raised beds, and play structures.

\nThe compact wheelbase combined with the rear-engine layout gives you a tighter turning radius than any front-engine lawn tractor in this size class. Five cutting heights from 1.5 to 3.75 inches cover the standard residential range. The single-lever cut-height adjustment is mounted within easy reach from the seat, so you're not hopping off to fine-tune.

\nWhere the CC30H really earns its place is in storage and access. Most homeowners shopping for their first riding mower realize too late that a 42-inch lawn tractor doesn't fit in a standard shed or pass through a typical fence opening. The CC30H is sized for that exact constraint. If you've been pushing for years because nothing else would fit, this is the one.

\nAvailable at Reyco Marine and Small Engine Ltd. in Sault Ste. Marie. column2: RM",4,"This is the mower I sell to people whose backyards have a fence. Standard lawn tractors are 42 to 50 inches wide and won't make it through a typical garden gate. The CC30H fits through 36 inches because the engine sits in the back instead of out front. That one design choice solves a real problem I see every spring. It's not the right machine if you're managing 2 acres of open lawn. For that, I'd put you on an XT1 or XT2. But if you're under an acre with tight access points, this beats pushing for an hour and a half. The hydrostatic transmission makes it dead simple to drive. I've sold this model to homeowners who've never operated a riding mower before, and they're cutting clean lines on day one. Compact, reliable, and priced right for what it is. If your lawn fits the use case, this is the answer.",57258,3099,3099,1B11TK60073,CC30H,,RM,"Riding
